summary: Govern an agent at runtime by intercepting each action and returning a verdict — ALLOW, ASK, or DENY — composed across policies in declared order, carrying per-session state, and failing closed.

rules.rule: A policy that throws DENIES by default — a broken guard never fails open; the only exceptions are a declared advisory/classifier-only policy failing to ALLOW and a declared approval-gate policy failing to ASK.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never apply writes before the human approves an ASK: approval is stateful, and a denied or timed-out ASK leaves no side effects — all writes are withheld.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Policies evaluate in declared order — a DENY short-circuits the chain, an ASK accumulates and withholds all writes until approval, an ALLOW continues — and the composed result names the deciding policy, stacked session → agent-spec → server/admin.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never gate on a vendor model id — gate on tier/capability; a brand-coupled threshold rots and fails the fire-test.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Where the static allowlist and the runtime engine both run, the static deny is the backstop and the engine the membrane — neither may dissolve the shared floor: the engine must never let through identity, destruction, secrets, or arbitrary-exec actions the static deny blocks.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
